chapters.notes: Opening, good news promised in Scriptures, Davidic seed and resurrection power, obedience of trust among nations, not ashamed, Divine righteousness/justice unveiled from trust to trust, wrath as unveiling against truth held down, idolatrous exchange, degraded social patterns. Includes safety note against weaponizing Romans 1 against sexual minorities.

Judging reader trap-door, kindness leading to turning/realignment, impartial judgment, doers of Law, nations showing Law-work in heart, Judean identity and hypocrisy critique, circumcision of heart in Spirit. Includes anti-replacement safety note.

Judean advantage and oracles, Divine trustworthiness, universal accountability, Law stopping every mouth, righteousness/justice of Divine apart from Law but witnessed by Law and Prophets, trust of/in Jesus Messiah, grace, redemption, mercy-seat/atoning sign, boasting excluded.

Abraham trusted and it was counted as righteousness/justice, David/Psalm 32 blessedness, circumcision as sign/seal after trust, Abraham as father of circumcised and uncircumcised trust, promise as grace, life from dead, raised Jesus.

Peace through being made righteous/just, access into grace, hope that does not shame, love poured through holy Spirit, reconciliation, Adam/Messiah contrast, sin/death/grace as reigning powers, grace overabounding.

Baptism into Messiah's death and resurrection life, old human crucified, sin/misalignment no longer ruling, body as instrument of righteousness/justice, under grace not permission, wages of sin and gift of life.
Law marriage analogy, release into newness of Spirit, Law as holy, sin/misalignment as parasitic power exploiting commandment, divided I, inner human delight in Law, rescue cry through Jesus Messiah.

No condemnation, Spirit of life, flesh-pattern versus Spirit-pattern, mortal bodies made alive, adoption/Abba, Spirit witness, creation groaning and birth pains, Spirit intercession, purpose/golden chain, no separation from Divine love in Messiah.

translation_notes: Romans should preserve Paul's dense argument without flattening it into later doctrinal slogans.
Pistis/pisteuo should generally be trust/trusting, with faith as glossary support where useful.
Dikaiosyne/dikaioo should preserve righteousness/justice/right-relatedness and being made righteous/just according to context.
Charis should be grace/gift-favor, not mere permission.
Hamartia should often be sin/misalignment, especially where Paul treats it as a power.
Sarx should be flesh, with notes distinguishing embodied creatureliness from domination by flesh-patterns.
Pneuma should preserve Spirit/breath resonance where context allows.
Nomos should be Law/law according to context, with Torah-sensitive notes where Paul speaks about Moses/Israel.
Ethne should be nations, not automatically Gentiles, unless context requires.
Do not make Romans anti-Jewish. Preserve Paul's Jewish identity, Israel concern, and complex Law/nations argument.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
Verse-number alignment must remain intact in both restored and mystical companion files.

Romans 1-4 establishes the argument's opening movement: good news as Divine power, righteousness/justice from trust to trust, idolatrous exchange, the judging-reader reversal, impartial judgment, universal accountability, grace apart from boasting, and Abraham as father of trust across circumcision/uncircumcision.

Romans 5-8 establishes the liberation core: peace and grace, hope without shame, love poured through Spirit, Adam/Messiah regime contrast, baptismal death/life participation, embodied justice, Law as holy and sin as parasitic power, no condemnation, Spirit adoption, creation groaning, Spirit intercession, and inseparable love.
next_actions: Continue Romans 9-11: Paul's grief for Israel, election/mercy, Potter imagery, remnant, stumbling stone, righteousness/justice and trust, word near you, Israel/nations, olive tree, mystery, all Israel, mercy on all, doxology.

Add glossary entries or reuse hooks: pistis, dikaiosyne, dikaioo, charis, hamartia, sarx, pneuma, nomos, ethnē, hilasterion, apolytrosis, logizomai, epangelia, Adam/Messiah, adoption, creation groaning, and no-separation language.
